heading: Project delivery process reforms
label: 2252 Project delivery process reforms
notes: References in TextThis subtitle, referred to in subsec. (b)(3), is subtitle A (§§ 2001–2013) of title II of , which enacted this section and sections 2253, 2254, and 2255 of this title, amended sections 2212, 2251, and 2327 of this title, and enacted provisions set out as a note under this section. L. 113–121, title II, § 2001128 Stat. 1260June 10, 2014“In this title [enacting this section and sections 2211a, 2238b, 2238c, 2243, 2253, 2254, and 2255 of this title and amending sections 2211, 2212, 2238, 2242, 2251, 2282a, and 2327 of this title and , Internal Revenue Code]:section 9505 of Title 26“(1).—Inland waterways trust fundThe term ‘Inland Waterways Trust Fund’ means the Inland Waterways Trust Fund established by section 9506(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [].26 U.S.C. 9506(a)“(2).—Qualifying projectThe term ‘qualifying project’ means any construction or major rehabilitation project for navigation infrastructure of the inland and intracoastal waterways that is—“(A)authorized before, on, or after the date of enactment of this Act [];June 10, 2014“(B)not completed on the date of enactment of this Act; and“(C)funded at least in part from the Inland Waterways Trust Fund.”

L. 113–121, title II, § 2001128 Stat. 1260June 10, 2014“In this title [enacting this section and sections 2211a, 2238b, 2238c, 2243, 2253, 2254, and 2255 of this title and amending sections 2211, 2212, 2238, 2242, 2251, 2282a, and 2327 of this title and , Internal Revenue Code]:section 9505 of Title 26“(1).—Inland waterways trust fundThe term ‘Inland Waterways Trust Fund’ means the Inland Waterways Trust Fund established by section 9506(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [].26 U.S.C. 9506(a)“(2).—Qualifying projectThe term ‘qualifying project’ means any construction or major rehabilitation project for navigation infrastructure of the inland and intracoastal waterways that is—“(A)authorized before, on, or after the date of enactment of this Act [];June 10, 2014“(B)not completed on the date of enactment of this Act; and“(C)funded at least in part from the Inland Waterways Trust Fund.”

children.label: A
children.text: the project manager have formal project management training and certification; and

children.label: B
children.text: the project manager be assigned from among personnel certified by the Chief of Engineers; and

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary utilize a risk-based cost estimate with a confidence level of at least 80 percent; and

children.label: i
children.text: for a qualifying project that requires an increase in the authorized amount in accordance with , during the preparation of a post-authorization change report or other similar decision document;section 2280 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: for a qualifying project for which the first construction contract has not been awarded, prior to the award of the first construction contract;

children.label: iii
children.text: for a qualifying project without a completed feasibility report in accordance with , prior to the completion of such a report; andsection 2282 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: for a qualifying project with a completed feasibility report in accordance with that has not yet been authorized, during design for the qualifying project.section 2282 of this title

children.heading: Additional project delivery process reforms
children.label: b Additional project delivery process reforms

children.text: establish a system to identify and apply on a continuing basis best management practices from prior or ongoing qualifying projects to improve the likelihood of on-time and on-budget completion of qualifying projects;
evaluate early contractor involvement acquisition procedures to improve on-time and on-budget project delivery performance; and

children.label: A
children.text: the implementation of applicable practices and procedures developed pursuant to management by the Secretary of an applicable military construction program;

children.label: B
children.text: the development and use of a portfolio of standard designs for inland navigation locks, incorporating the use of a center of expertise for the design and review of qualifying projects;

children.label: C
children.text: the use of full-funding contracts or formulation of a revised continuing contracts clause; and

children.label: D
children.text: the establishment of procedures for recommending new project construction starts using a capital projects business model.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the Secretary may carry out pilot projects to evaluate processes and procedures for the study, design, and construction of qualifying projects.

children.label: A
children.text: early contractor involvement in the development of features and components;

children.label: B
children.text: an appropriate use of continuing contracts for the construction of features and components; and

children.label: C
children.text: applicable principles, procedures, and processes used for military construction projects.
